Tags – Places

Tags – Pastimes

Maharaj conversation with West Virginia University scientist, Arjuna bird eye piercing, Satyabhama weighs Krishna with her Jewels

Tags – Persons

Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura, Krishna, Srila Prabhupada, Dronacharya, Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhima, Arjuna, Satyabhama, Rukmini, Narad muni

Tags – Themes

Perceptions, determination, desire, guru’s words, faith

vyavasāyātmikā buddhir
ekeha kuru-nandana
bahu-śākhā hy anantāś ca
buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām

[Bg 2.41]

Translation

Those who are on this path are resolute in purpose, and their aim is one. O beloved child of the Kurus, the intelligence of those who are irresolute is many-branched.

Purport

A strong faith that by Kṛṣṇa consciousness one will be elevated to the highest perfection of life is called vyavasāyātmikā intelligence. The Caitanya-caritāmṛta (Madhya 22.62) states:

‘śraddhā’-śabde – viśvāsa kahe sudṛḍha niścaya
kṛṣṇe bhakti kaile sarva-karma kṛta haya

Faith means unflinching trust in something sublime. When one is engaged in the duties of Kṛṣṇa consciousness, he need not act in relationship to the material world with obligations to family traditions, humanity or nationality. Fruitive activities are the engagements of one’s reactions from past good or bad deeds. When one is awake in Kṛṣṇa consciousness, he need no longer endeavor for good results in his activities. When one is situated in Kṛṣṇa consciousness, all activities are on the absolute plane, for they are no longer subject to dualities like good and bad. The highest perfection of Kṛṣṇa consciousness is renunciation of the material conception of life. This state is automatically achieved by progressive Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

The resolute purpose of a person in Kṛṣṇa consciousness is based on knowledge. Vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ: a person in Kṛṣṇa consciousness is the rare good soul who knows perfectly that Vāsudeva, or Kṛṣṇa, is the root of all manifested causes. As by watering the root of a tree one automatically distributes water to the leaves and branches, so by acting in Kṛṣṇa consciousness one can render the highest service to everyone – namely self, family, society, country, humanity, etc. If Kṛṣṇa is satisfied by one’s actions, then everyone will be satisfied.

Service in Kṛṣṇa consciousness is, however, best practiced under the able guidance of a spiritual master who is a bona fide representative of Kṛṣṇa, who knows the nature of the student and who can guide him to act in Kṛṣṇa consciousness. As such, to be well versed in Kṛṣṇa consciousness one has to act firmly and obey the representative of Kṛṣṇa, and one should accept the instruction of the bona fide spiritual master as one’s mission in life. Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura instructs us, in his famous prayers for the spiritual master, as follows:

yasya prasādād bhagavat-prasādo
yasyāprasādān na gatiḥ kuto ’pi
dhyāyan stuvaṁs tasya yaśas tri-sandhyaṁ
vande guroḥ śrī-caraṇāravindam

“By satisfaction of the spiritual master, the Supreme Personality of Godhead becomes satisfied. And by not satisfying the spiritual master, there is no chance of being promoted to the plane of Kṛṣṇa consciousness. I should, therefore, meditate and pray for his mercy three times a day, and offer my respectful obeisances unto him, my spiritual master.”

The whole process, however, depends on perfect knowledge of the soul beyond the conception of the body – not theoretically but practically, when there is no longer a chance for sense gratification manifested in fruitive activities. One who is not firmly fixed in mind is diverted by various types of fruitive acts.

Lecture Transcription

[Maharaj reads Translation and Purport]

Determination is essential for advancement in spiritual life

Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura has commented in regard to this verse that, “Determination comes when we make the desire of Guru our life and soul.” Krishna is explaining that, “Those who are on this path are resolute in purpose and their aim is one. Those who are not on this path, their intelligence is many branched.” Strength comes when there is focus on what is supporting our lives.

Just like a ship, when a ship is on the sea where there are many waves, sometimes it is stormy, sometimes it is quite peaceful. Sometimes during a storm the ship has to stop and put an anchor to the ground below. That anchor gives the focus point of security to the ship. But when that anchor is drawn then waves can tossed the ship in any direction and the ship has no power to protect itself. So similarly we must keep our anchor fully fixed in our faith in Guru and Krishna. Then despite the multifarious circumstances that come in front of our life we remain steady, we remain fixed. So Krishna is explaining here that, “That anchor, that determination, that resolution of purpose, is the essential ingredients for advancement of devotional service.” After all Maya is everywhere in this material existence and in everything she is inviting us to divert our attention away from God.

daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī
mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante
māyām etāṁ taranti te

[Bg. 7.14]

[This divine energy of Mine, consisting of the three modes of material nature, is difficult to overcome. But those who have surrendered unto Me can easily cross beyond it.]

Maya is very powerful. In fact Krishna says she is impossible to overcome unless you surrendered to him. What does it mean to surrender to him? It means to have that resolute determination that, “Krishna your will is my will. Let your will be done.” And of course the will of Krishna comes through the spiritual master. Our connection comes to be instruction, the merciful order of Guru Dev. So therefore if we fixed our mind, if we fixed our senses in executing that instruction, that is the anchor that keeps us stable even in these ocean of material existence and when the storm come in the form of crises in our life, in the form of opposition from our enemies, in the form of opposition from our friends and God Brothers, in the form of disappointments, in the form of sensual temptations, in the form of apparent confusions and bewilderment that come before us in life, we always remain steady and undisturbed. Otherwise if we do not have this fixed purpose we are easily bewildered.

prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni
guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ
ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā
kartāham iti manyate

[Bg. 3.27]

[The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.]

That the condition soul being bewildered, always being bewildered by the three modes of material nature thinks himself to be doer of his activities, which are actually simply being carried out by nature. So greatest disturbances in our spiritual progress is when we become bewildered and that is how Maya is constantly trying to take our attention away from Krishna. If she can bewilder us then that resolute determination cannot be there, and without that resolute determination she can easily draw our mind, draw our senses, draw our intelligence away, away from Guru and Krishna. When we are bewildered, then naturally we are subjected to considering so many other alternatives. Haa..? Therefore the basis of Krishna consciousness is faith and faith is the result of determination. Faith is not a cheap thing. We have to cultivate determination.

Words of Guru and Krishna is Real intelligence

I remember in 1972 Srila Prabhupada at New Vrindavan was describing to us the importance of determination in a devotee. And one person asked, raised his hand and said, “Srila Prabhupada what if we just do not have any determination for devotional service?”

And Srila Prabhupada gave a very famous answer he said, “If you have no determination then you are an animal. The difference between a human and an animal is that a human being has determination and an animal simply follows the dictations of the mind and senses. But a human being had that determinant... that facility to have a determined will to do what is right, despite the temptations of mind and senses.”

Krishna says in a third chapter of Gita, “One must conquer lower self with higher self. One must conquer the mind with the intelligence.” And what is the intelligence? The intelligence is the word of Guru and word of Krishna. Outside of that we have no real intelligence. In the school of bhakti, intelligence means, “Simply to accept the word of Krishna and the word of Guru as the authority in my life.” What was our Arjuna’s intelligence? He was…he was very bewildered. Was he not? As long as his own mind was trying to figure out what is the right course of action. His intelligence was simply to accept Krishna; his intelligence was whatever Krishna told him, his intelligence was not what he figured out. And in spiritual life our intelligence is not what we create, our intelligence is when we accept, when we accept the word of God from the Guru, Sadhu and Shastra. That, those words are our guiding force in life.

But Maya is giving so many other alternatives. Therefore Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura has said that, “This resolute determination means simply to make the order of Guru your life and soul. To have that faith.” Rupa Goswami explains, “The sublime faith devotee must have is that, “If I simply surrendered to Krishna my life will be perfect, all desire will be fulfilled, there will be nothing that remains to be accomplished in my life.”” Prabhupada here gives a wonderful definition, “Faith means unflinching trust in something sublime.” And that sublime, something that Prabhupada is talking about, is something that is beyond our sense perception.

Our faith must be beyond sense perception

The great Acharya generally do not perform their miracles, because for the sincere class of man miracles are more a disturbance than an asset in the development one… development of one’s devotional qualities. Because if your faith is in hearing the word of God then you have faith in what is beyond your senses perception. And God is beyond your sense perception. If you want to understand God you have to have faith in what is beyond your senses perception. Correct? But if you are in need of some physical proof that you can witness with your mind and senses to confirm your faith, then your faith is constantly being restricted and limited to what you can perceive, to what is within the preview of your sense grati…over sense perception.

If someone says that, “I am representing God and I can prove it. Here I can create a Mridanga, I can create a flower, I can walk on the water.” Then for everything he says you will be depending on some visual proof and that is limitation. Therefore Lord Jesus in the Bible its describe he performed so many miracles to the very less intelligent class of people but then he went to, came to one village and they did not ask him for any miracles they just asked him to instruct and he spoke and he was so pleased, he said, “These people.” He said, “They do not need these [00:16:13 unclear] to believe in me.” He appreciated that they were most sincere. So our faith has to be what is beyond our sense perception.

I remember one time, I was in West Virginia University regularly lecturing, Morgantown, West Virginia and one man who was getting his Masters degree in medical technology, he was becoming very serious about devotional service but he was a scientist, he was trained as a scientist in a colleges of America for many, many years, and he was very, very scrutinizing, analytical, intelligent man and he was reading the fifth canto of Srimad Bhagavatam in which Sukadev Goswami is describing the phenomenon of material nature very much different than our modern scientist describe. So he was very bewildered, “How could this be true? I cannot accept that this is true and if I do not accept even one part of Bhagavatam, I have to reject the whole thing.” He came… What happened is, I was out of town and he went to the temple president and he was completely confused, he was already chanting sixteen rounds, following four regulative principles. When he read this he stopped chanting. He was ready to just, he was crying, he was weeping, “I thought I found a spiritual movement I could surrender to and here I am finding a conflict that I cannot accept; because the Bhagavatam if it is actually word of God, I have to accept every part of it in toto. I can’t accept some and some not, otherwise that is not god. If I cannot accept this I have to throw out the rest.” So when I came we spent the whole night talking, I said, “Let us go to New Vrin... [00:18:09 – 00:18:50 Audio interrupted and repeated] “The Srimad Bhagavatam is discussing what is beyond your sense perception and the scientist can only explain what is within your sense perception. Now this is the test of your faith. Are you going to put your faith in the Sadhus in the parampara system, in the… in the Shastra and in the word of God or you are going to put your faith in the scientists who are in this material field of sense perception and cannot even conceive of anything beyond. If you need for the scientist to confirm whatever God says then what is your faith in God? “Yes I believe Krishna is…is correct, I believe the scripture is correct because scientists have proved it.” Huh?”

Order of Guru should be life and soul

Do you require the confirmation of the materialists? Sometimes certain lecturers when…when some very, very inconceivable explanation of the scripture is there, they try to justify how actually this is perfectly in accordance with that, what scientists have discovered themselves. Huh? You must choose who is the authority. Your faith has to be in what is sublime beyond your sense beside, beyond your sense perception. And this faith is the basis of our spiritual advancement. Because this faith keeps us secure in our convictions in a world that is filled by doubt, suspicion and bewilderment. And how to preserve our faith? Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura says, “By making the order of your Guru your life and soul.” Be resolute in purpose, resolute, fixed. “I want to be Krishna consciousness, I want to serve my Guru.” And you have to have such intense and strong fine tuning to that goal in life and nothing else will deviate or disturbed from that, that is what we require to be Krishna consciousness. It is described in haa... Mahabharata that Arjuna, Yudhiṣṭhira, Nakula, Bhima, Sahadev they were all the disciples of Dronacharya in the military field. So Dronacharya, one time he wanted to test them. So after completing their education he put an imitation bird in the tree, high in the tree and told the five of them that, “I want you to take your arrow and pierce the eye of that bird.” So first Yudhiṣṭhira came up. And Drona was standing next to him and said, “Now take your bow and take your arrow and aim it toward that bird.” Yudhiṣṭhira aimed.

Drona said, “What do you see?”

He said that, “I see the bird.”

“Do you see anything else?” He said, “I see the leaves and the branches of the tree.” Yudhiṣṭhira said, “Go away.”

I mean Dronacharya said, “Go away.” Yudhiṣṭhira went away.

Then Drona calls Bhima. Drona said, “Take your aim.” Bhima took his aim.

Drona said, “What you see?”

He said, “I see the bird.”

“Do you see anything else?”

He said, “I see… I see all of you. I see the tree. I see the branches. I see the leaves.”

Drona said, “Go away.” And then he called for Arjuna. He told Arjuna, “Take your aim.” He took his aim.

“What did you see?”

“I see the bird.”

He said, “I see the…. “He said, “I see the eye of the bird.”

“Do you see anything else?”

He said, “No!”

He said, “Did you see the trees?”

He said, “I don’t see the tree.”

He said, “Did you see the bird?”

He said, “I don’t see the bird.”

He says, “ Did you see the head of the bird?”

He says, “I do not see the head of the bird, I only see the eye.”

Drona said, “Then you shoot.” and Arjuna pierced the eye of the bird.

If you want to be Krishna consciousness you have to become like this, if you are looking any other direction or seeing anything else you are not qualified. This is what is called resolute purpose and determination. This is what is called fixing your mind in the order of Guru and Krishna. Nothing is impossible for one who has this faith in Guru and Krishna. This is the path those who follow are resolute in purpose, and their aim is one.

Guru’s words outweighs everything else

“O beloved child of the Kurus, the intelligence of those who are irresolute is many-branched.” Our goal is the Lotus feet of Krishna which is coming through the instruction of Gurudev. Guru means heavy, that means everyone in the world has various opinions but Guru’s opinion outweighs everyone else's opinion. Haaa? This is what it means when we say Guru is heavy. It doesn’t mean Guru is big in size, it means that Guru’s word. Guru doesn’t mean a physical body Shabda Brahma, our parampara is a parampara of sound vibration not physical body. The physical body is simply the instrument in which sound vibration comes through. Just like if there is a particular radio station that is broadcasting… broadcasting a certain message whether you have Sony tape recorder, or whether you have Panasonic tape recorder, or whether you have a National tape recorder, or whether you have a, Umm! What other tape recorders do you have?

[Devotee:] Philips.

[Maharaj:] Philips! Even if you have a Philips tape recorder. Whether it is a cheap tape recorder, or whether it is a radio, or whether it is an exclusive expensive radio. If the message is coming through, that is what is important. We are not concerned with the form or the shape as we are of the message. This is Parampara. The parampara is, the Guru can be white color the Guru can be black color, the Guru can be big, the Guru can be small, the Guru could be a woman, the Guru could be a man. The spirit of parampara is that the message, the sound is the same. And that sound vibration is the word of God which is coming through the transparent via media, the spiritual master. Guru, Sadhu, Shastras is the means by which the sound vibration of god’s word, that’s entering into the domain of material existence. So when we fix our consciousness on instruction of Guru Dev, when we fix all of our assets in life on the instruction of Guru Dev, when our faith is exclusively in the order of Guru Dev then we understand what it means that Guru is heavy. Krishna is heavier than the heaviest and his words are non- different than him. That is why we say Guru is heavy because he is only repeating the word of God. Guru is heavy and Krishna is heavy but he is outweighed by submissive humble devotion. Haa?

Only Bhakti can conquer God

There is a beautiful story that when Krishna was living in Dwarka, Narad Muni came Huh! And he decided that Krishna and he should travel to some distant destination and of course the queens of Dwarka were very unhappy with it. So Rukmini, Satyabhama, they were saying, “No, no, no please do not take Krishna away.” So they said that, “We…” So Narad Muni and Krishna decided that if they could give Narad Muni... What was it? Krishna’s weight in gold? Huh? [00:29:28 – 00:29:30 not clear] Equal… equal gold then Narad Muni would take the gold and they could keep Krishna. So Satyabhama took all of the gold and ornaments, Krishna was sitting on one side of the scale, she put all other ornaments on the other side of the scale and Krishna was more heavy. Because after all what can outweigh God? When we say Guru is heavy; Guru is the sound vibration of god’s words, because God is heavy Guru is heavy. But they could not, Satyabhama was very sad, “How we will… how will we keep Krishna? All my ornaments all my gold, and the scale is not even growing the slightest bit up on Krishna’s side.” Satyabham… Rukmini came and she put one Tulsi leaf on the other side of the scale and the whole scale went [Maharaj makes whooping sound] and Krishna went way up in the air. What does this mean? That bhakti is the only power that can conquer God; we can purchase Krishna with bhakti. Similarly Guru is heavy but devotion to Guru can even purchase the mercy, the divine grace of the spiritual master.

So these must be our determination, this must be our resolute purpose in life mission of Guru Dev. And we cannot calculate according to the material considerations, we must calculate according to the transcendental vision of the divine authorities of Krishna consciousness. And if our faith is in this way and our determination is in this way and our mind and our senses are determinately fixed in this way then we... the path of Krishna consciousness is the path that we are following in life and the result is guaranteed.

man-manā bhava mad-bhakto
mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru
mām evaiṣyasi yuktvaivam
ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ

[Bg. 9.34]

[Engage your mind always in thinking of Me, become My devotee, offer obeisances to Me and worship Me. Being completely absorbed in Me, surely you will come to Me.]

“One who with this determination always thinks of me, becomes my devotee, worships me and offers homage onto me, such a person will come to me without fail.” So Krishna consciousness is not cheap. The price of Krishna consciousness is this determination, this faith and really that is the only price, that is the only cost.

Any Questions?

Q&A Transcription

[Devotee:] Maharaj you said about the Arjuna he used to have only the direction of seeing the eyes of the parrot. That basis we should also have only one determination how to serve the Guru service that Krishna consciousness movement is our life is completely for Guru. It can be about myself my own experience is, one day we used to have hundred percent faith on Guru, another day comes down or it become shaken. How to see that hundred percent faith is constant and that will be forever?

[Maharaj:] This is what it means to conquer the lower self with the higher self. You must act according to your intelligence. This is how our faith is developed. Faith is…pure faith is a very advanced platform. We should not think, we are in such advanced platform. We have to cultivate that seed of faith to bring into that advance platform and this is the process that we have to accept things favorable for the development of that faith and reject those things unfavorable. We must associate with persons who have cultivated that faith and we should try to avoid as far as possible the association of those persons who disturb that faith. Huh? And in our actions of life we must act with that determination based on intelligence that, “Guru is saying this but my mind and senses are saying so many other things.”, Right? That means you don’t have complete faith. If you had complete faith your mind and senses would be perfectly united with the word of Guru. Right? That’s what you are saying that there is conflict of interest in your own mind. So determination means, “Whatever my mind and senses tell me I will do what Guru wants. I will live for that.” Huh? When Arjuna was fixing his vision on the eye of that bird does not mean that all those other things did not exist they were there but he was…he was keeping his mind fixed on only that, and that is an austerity, that is the tapasya, that is sacrifice. So many other beautiful things were there around the forest, so many charming amm... items within the atmosphere but he was only concerned with that bird’s eye, because that was the order of the Guru. Huh? So this conflict of interest that, “My Guru wants this but my mind and senses and everyone else is proposing so many other alternatives. That means there must be a sacrifice, that means there must be tapasya. But I will put aside all these other things but the one real thing ‘my say… my service to Guru and Krishna.’” Haa?

This determination is a sacrifice. And sacrifice is not a sacrifice if it’s easy if it has no other alternatives, what makes the sacrifice a sacrifice is that there are so many other alternatives, there are so many other temptations but you put them all aside and focus only on this. Now that everyone has the power to do. That is what Prabhupada meant when he said, “If you don’t have determination that means you are an animal.” Human being… Human life is meant for tapasya.

tapo divyaṁ putrakā yena sattvaṁ
śuddhyed yasmād brahma-saukhyaṁ tv anantam

[SB 5.5.1]

That, “Now you have attained this human life, it is meant for tapasya.” And what is that tapasya this determination, your mind may be telling you so many things that means you may not complete faith. But you are determined that, “This is the direction I will go.” Haa? And by that constant practice with this determination gradually that faith become very strong, it becomes very real and through that process

ādau śraddhā tataḥ sādhu-
saṅgo ’tha bhajana-kriyā
tato ’nartha-nivṛttiḥ syāt
tato niṣṭhā rucis tataḥ

[CC Madhya 23.14]

[“ ‘In the beginning there must be faith. Then one becomes interested in associating with pure devotees. Thereafter one is initiated by the spiritual master and executes the regulative principles under his orders. Thus one is freed from all unwanted habits and becomes firmly fixed in devotional service. Thereafter, one develops taste and attachment. This is the way of sādhana-bhakti, the execution of devotional service according to the regulative principles. Gradually emotions intensify, and finally there is an awakening of love. This is the gradual development of love of Godhead for the devotee interested in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.’]

Rupa Goswami says, “In the beginning it is little faith ādau śraddhā. From that faith Krishna directs you to the association of devotees. In that association you hear from them. Through hearing your faith gets stronger, as your faith develops bhajana-kriyā you accept the principle of devotional service, you accept a spiritual master, you obey his instruction then your faith grows from that process. And as your faith grows, anartha-nivṛttiḥ, all the unwanted… all the unwanted ideas and thoughts within your mind are cleansed out and then niṣṭhā then your faith become strong, very strong un…unshakable, resolute Huh? So in the process, it is a tapasya. The final result is not tapasya; it has a higher taste. It is simply sweet and pleasing to make Guru’s order your life and soul. That’s nothing else that even is slightest bit desirable to you except the pleasure of Guru and Krishna. But in the beginning it is a tapasya, it is a sacrifice, to willingly volunteering put everything else aside for that.

Any other questions? All the great saints are here tonight Bhakti Yog Maharaj, Vrindavan Maharaj, Devamrita Prabhu, Satyavrat [Maharaj laughs] There is no more questions? Yes”

[Devotee:] How to put false ego on back seat?

[Maharaj:] How to put false ego on back seat? Actually real question is how to throw him out of car? [Maharaj laughs] Because even if he is in a back seat it is something called back seat driver. [Maharaj laughs] The same process. If you don’t listen it is said, “If an unwanted guest comes, do not give him any food and he will leave.” Right? [Maharaj laughs] When an unwanted guest comes and you feed him sumptuously then he is going to stay rest of his life. [Maharaj laughs] He will say, “Why should I go home? This is better.” But if your unwanted guest comes you give no food and he will leave. He will think ‘I will go somewhere else. There is nothing here for me.” So this is how we also get rid of false ego. As long as you are serving him, as long as you are serving your ego, your ego will, you feed ego by listening to it, this is the food for the ego, submission. So whether the ego is in the front seat or back seat, as long as you are listening to its advice, he is there right in your car. He will take you, he will force you to drive him wherever he wants to go, but if you don’t listen to him then he will have to leave sooner or later. And how do we avoid listening?

vyavasāyātmikā buddhir
ekeha kuru-nandana
bahu-śākhā hy anantāś ca
buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām

[BG 2.41]

[Those who are on this path are resolute in purpose, and their aim is one. O beloved child of the Kurus, the intelligence of those who are irresolute is many-branched.]

To make our order of Guru our life and soul. If we have no other object in our life to fulfill except the order of Guru, then the… if you are on that platform then your false ego will starve. He will say, “Stop the car, I am going somewhere else.” [Devotees laughs] Haa? Does that answer your question? Actually this one verse answers every question. Whatever question you ask, we can answer the question with this verse. Because with resolute determination when our aim is one, when we make the order of Guru our life and soul every other problem is solved by this simple process. Every question can be answered not theoretically but through realization if we simply apply this principle alone. It is the essence of spiritual life. Srila Madhavendra Puri prayed, “My lord Shri Krishna is that my, since I have surrounded to you, my family members think I am mad, the society thinks I am crazy, my nation has rejected me, people laugh at me but I do not care what any opinion is because you are satisfied you are pleased with my service.” That is resolute determination, that means aim is one.

Thank you very much.
